sites.name: Fresno
sites.note: Operational HQ. Central Valley hub, established base, massive inland CA housing demand.

sites.name: Rosamond
sites.note: Kern County / Antelope Valley. Inexpensive land, LA Basin overflow buyers, Edwards AFB workforce housing.

sites.name: Corona
sites.note: SoCal Hub HQ becomes franchise site. Inland Empire is ground zero for CA ADU demand — by-right state law, severe shortage, anchors West Coast network.

sites.name: San Diego
sites.note: One of the highest ADU permit rates in the nation. Streamlined permitting, military workforce housing, strong homeowner ROI story, short haul from Corona.

sites.name: Stockton
sites.note: Bay Area price refugee corridor. CA ADU law fully applies, large multigenerational homeowner base, extends Fresno coverage north through San Joaquin Valley.

sites.name: Honolulu
sites.note: HI ADU law among the most aggressive in the US. Extreme land scarcity, highest housing costs nationally, modular ships well to islands — unique captive market.

sites.name: Phoenix
sites.note: Largest Sun Belt growth market. Massive population influx, tech/finance relocations, gateway to AZ franchise network. City-level ADU rules permissive despite mixed state legislature.

sites.name: Hartford
sites.note: Northeast Hub launch. CT ADU legislation 2021 — statewide by-right. Anchors MA, RI, NY network. Insurance/finance workforce, high homeowner equity.

sites.name: Denver
sites.note: CO statewide ADU law 2024 — strongest in Mountain West. By-right ADU required in all cities. Rapid growth, tech culture buyer, anchors CO hub for Fort Collins and Colorado Springs.

sites.name: Las Vegas
sites.note: NV ADU reform + massive CA overflow demand. Tens of thousands of LA/OC price refugees — exact modular buyer. Workforce housing shortage severe. Short haul from Corona hub.

sites.name: Austin
sites.note: Eliminated single-family zoning citywide 2023. ADU permitting streamlined city-wide. Tech boom workforce housing gap. Pulled from Yr 4 — first-mover opportunity now.

sites.name: Boston
sites.note: MA MBTA Communities Act mandates density near transit statewide. Severe housing shortage, high-income educated buyer, strong sustainability ethos. Hartford hub live — extend north now.

sites.name: Portland
sites.note: Oregon pioneered the nation's first statewide ADU legalization. HB 2001 eliminated single-family exclusionary zoning. Highest modular/prefab awareness outside CA. Pulled from Yr 5.

sites.name: Seattle
sites.note: WA ADU reform + missing middle housing laws statewide. Extreme cost crisis, tech workforce demand. Top-3 national ADU market by permit volume. Pulled from Yr 5.

sites.name: Salt Lake City
sites.note: UT ADU by-right law 2021 — statewide. Fastest-growing metro corridor, young family demographic, tech migration from CA. Denver hub covers easily. Pulled from Yr 5.

sites.name: Boise
sites.note: One of the most acute housing cost explosions in the US 2019-2024. CA refugee buyers, affordability crisis, ID ADU-permissive. Demand is now. Pulled from Yr 5.

sites.name: Charlotte
sites.note: Charlotte UDO upzoning 2022 — most progressive zoning overhaul in the Southeast. Rapid population growth, finance/tech hub. Zoning reform window is now. Pulled from Yr 5.

sites.name: Raleigh
sites.note: Research Triangle tech/biotech workforce housing demand. By-right ADU reform active. Charlotte + Raleigh together justify a Southeast hub. Pulled from Yr 5.

sites.name: Fort Collins
sites.note: CO ADU law applies. CSU university market drives year-round rental ADU demand. Sustainability-oriented buyers. Short haul from Denver hub — low incremental cost.

sites.name: New Haven
sites.note: CT ADU legislation 2021 — statewide by-right. Yale institutional market, high homeowner equity, urban infill demand. Pairs with Bridgeport to cover CT shoreline.

sites.name: Bridgeport
sites.note: CT legislation + NYC commuter overflow. Fairfield County ADU demand from homeowners offsetting high property costs. Strong rental ADU ROI. Pairs with New Haven as CT push.

sites.name: Providence
sites.note: RI ADU reform active. RISD/Brown buyer culture leans modular. Boston overflow demand. Hartford hub proximity makes this low-cost to add. Early mover advantage.

sites.name: Jersey City
sites.note: NJ ADU law passed 2024. NYC overflow — homeowners with massive equity seeking income-generating ADUs. High demand volume compensates for higher land cost.

sites.name: Dallas
sites.note: Largest TX metro, anchors Dallas distribution hub serving Austin, Houston, SA cluster. ADU awareness growing. Sheer market size demands presence even with car-centric sprawl culture.

sites.name: Colorado Springs
sites.note: CO ADU law applies. Large military workforce — Peterson, Schriever, Fort Carson. Military ADU demand is financeable (VA-compatible). Constrained footprint drives ADU appeal.

sites.name: Reno
sites.note: NV ADU reform + Bay Area/Tesla Gigafactory workforce overflow. Massive housing cost gap vs SF. Tech manufacturing workforce needs affordable options.

sites.name: Minneapolis
sites.note: Minneapolis eliminated single-family zoning — first major US city to do so. Strong modular awareness, sustainability culture. Landmark reform market pulled from Yr 8.

sites.name: Atlanta
sites.note: Intown ADU reform active. Film/tech hub workforce housing need. Largest SE metro — justifies standalone coverage. ADU adoption growing among Beltline corridor homeowners.

sites.name: Tucson
sites.note: AZ city-level ADU rules permissive despite state legislature friction. University of Arizona market, retiree casita demand, Phoenix-overflow affordability seekers.

sites.name: Houston
sites.note: No zoning = permissive by default. Sheer market size necessary. Energy sector workforce, large lot inventory for ADU placement. Sprawl culture is a headwind but volume compensates.

sites.name: Albany
sites.note: NY ADU reform active statewide. State capital — stable government/healthcare employment. Hartford hub proximity makes this low-cost. Anchors upstate NY corridor.

sites.name: Buffalo
sites.note: NY reform + rust belt revival. Affordability crisis severe. Legacy housing stock generates strong ADU-as-income-property demand. Low land cost = strong modular ROI.

sites.name: Syracuse
sites.note: NY reform. Syracuse University rental ADU demand. Micron chip factory creating new workforce housing need — major tailwind. Affordable land makes modular ROI compelling for investors.

sites.name: Manchester
sites.note: NH ADU legislation 2017. Boston overflow market. No state income tax draws remote workers creating demand spike. Small but consistent.

sites.name: Portland
sites.note: ME ADU reform active. Remote worker boom drove housing costs sharply up. Density-aware sustainability buyer. Low competition — early mover captures the whole market.

sites.name: Trenton
sites.note: NJ ADU law 2024. More affordable land than Jersey City while inside NYC commuter shed. Urban renewal opportunity. Completes NJ market alongside Jersey City.

sites.name: Worcester
sites.note: MA policy excellent but secondary to Boston. Establish Boston Yr 3, expand west Yr 5. WPI/Clark University ADU rental demand. Better modular ROI than Boston. Pushed from Yr 3.

sites.name: Newark
sites.note: NJ ADU law applies. Let Jersey City establish NJ brand first. Newark has real demand — strong long-term pipeline. Pushed from Yr 3.

sites.name: Rochester
sites.note: NY reform. Kodak/Paychex/U of R workforce stable. Flat population = lower demand velocity. Enter after NY reform matures and upstate market is primed. Pushed from Yr 3.

sites.name: Nashville
sites.note: Fastest-growing TN city. Healthcare/tech hub, ADU reform accelerating. Moved to Yr 5 from Yr 7 — TN legislation moving faster than expected.

sites.name: San Antonio
sites.note: Large military market — Fort Sam Houston, Lackland, Randolph AFB. VA-financeable ADU demand is consistent and countercyclical. Completes TX triangle.

sites.name: Richmond
sites.note: VA ADU reform advancing. State capital stability. Growing tech sector, Amazon HQ2 spillover. Bridges DC Suburbs to the Carolinas.

sites.name: Omaha
sites.note: Berkshire/finance hub, stable employment base, affordable land. NE ADU environment improving. Midwest market proving ground.

sites.name: Oklahoma City
sites.note: OK ADU environment slowly improving. Energy sector workforce. Affordable land = compelling modular ROI for investors. Dallas hub efficient.

sites.name: Albuquerque
sites.note: NM ADU-permissive environment. Growing tech/gov sector, Kirtland AFB workforce housing. Affordable land, constrained supply. Denver hub covers geographically.

sites.name: Savannah
sites.note: SCAD arts/design culture = modular-receptive buyer. Port of Savannah workforce housing demand growing rapidly. Bridges Atlanta and FL coastal coverage.

sites.name: Baton Rouge
sites.note: LA ADU environment improving. LSU university, petrochemical industry workforce housing. Dallas hub covers efficiently.

sites.name: Miami
sites.note: FL ADU rules — Miami permissive. Extreme housing cost pressure, international buyer base, investor-oriented ADU demand. Anchor for FL franchise network.

sites.name: Orlando
sites.note: Tourism/hospitality workforce housing need. Rapid population growth. Disney/Universal workforce creates consistent rental ADU demand.

sites.name: Tampa
sites.note: FL Gulf Coast growth corridor. Remote worker influx drove housing costs sharply. Finance/tech relocation hub.

sites.name: Jacksonville
sites.note: FL, large military presence — NAS Jax, Mayport. Military ADU demand steady and VA-financeable. Largest land-area US city — huge lot inventory for ADU infill.

sites.name: Chicago
sites.note: Chicago ADU pilot expanded citywide 2023. Deep two-flat/three-flat housing culture aligns with ADU concept. High homeowner equity in established neighborhoods.

sites.name: Indianapolis
sites.note: Midwest hub city — hub becomes a franchise site. Affordable land, steady workforce growth. Lowest incremental cost market to launch given hub operations already in place.

sites.name: Baltimore
sites.note: MD ADU legislation advancing. DC metro overflow, large rowhouse inventory ideal for ADU. Healthcare/government workforce stability.

sites.name: DC Suburbs
sites.note: MD/VA ADU-friendly jurisdictions, federal workforce stability. Montgomery County strong ADU permitting. High homeowner equity drives income-generating ADU demand.

sites.name: Columbus
sites.note: Fastest-growing OH city. Intel fab investment creating new workforce housing demand. Ohio State market. Anchors OH cluster.

sites.name: Cleveland
sites.note: OH market. Healthcare/Case Western anchor. Rust belt revival creates ADU-as-investment demand. Affordable land = strong modular ROI.

sites.name: Cincinnati
sites.note: OH/KY market. P&G/finance workforce stability. Growing tech sector. Completes Indianapolis hub OH coverage.

sites.name: Philadelphia
sites.note: PA ADU rules locally variable — Philly moving toward reform. Large rowhouse stock, high homeowner equity, major university market.

sites.name: Pittsburgh
sites.note: PA tech revival — CMU/Pitt robotics hub. Google, Uber, Duolingo workforce creates housing demand. Affordable land = strong modular ROI.

sites.name: Harrisburg
sites.note: PA state capital — government workforce stability. Bridges coverage between Philly and Pittsburgh markets.

sites.name: Louisville
sites.note: KY ADU environment improving. Healthcare/UofL anchor market. Close Indianapolis hub proximity keeps costs low.

sites.name: Kansas City
sites.note: MO market improving. Tech hub growth, affordable land. Animal health/veterinary sector unique workforce demand.

sites.name: St. Louis
sites.note: Population stabilizing. Healthcare/Wash U anchor. Enter once KC franchise proves the MO model.

sites.name: New Orleans
sites.note: LA ADU improving, historic shotgun house culture already primes the ADU concept culturally. Tourism/port workforce. Unique architectural market.

sites.name: Birmingham
sites.note: AL market maturing slowly. UAB anchor. Enter when Indianapolis hub reaches full capacity and SE coverage gap needs closing.

sites.name: Huntsville
sites.note: Rocket/defense tech workforce — NASA, Redstone Arsenal, Boeing. Stable high-income housing demand. Innovation-receptive buyer strongly suited to modular.

sites.name: Wilmington
sites.note: DE ADU-friendly environment, corporate/legal industry workforce. Proximity to Philly and DC extends Northeast coverage efficiently.

sites.name: Ft Lauderdale
sites.note: FL South corridor. Extends Miami coverage north. High homeowner equity in established beach communities.

sites.name: Detroit
sites.note: MI ADU reform active. EV manufacturing revival (Ford, GM, Rivian) creating new workforce housing demand. Enter if MI legislation moves by 2027.

sites.name: Grand Rapids
sites.note: West MI furniture/manufacturing hub. Conservative homeowner base responds to property-investment ADU framing. Growing healthcare sector.

sites.name: Charleston
sites.note: SC growth corridor, Joint Base Charleston military presence. High average home value = compelling ADU ROI story.

sites.name: Greenville
sites.note: Fastest-growing SC city. BMW/Michelin/tech manufacturing workforce housing demand. Upstate SC corridor pairs with Charlotte.

sites.name: Memphis
sites.note: TN market. FedEx logistics hub, large workforce. Affordable land = strong modular ROI for investors. Pairs with Nashville to complete TN coverage.

sites.name: Knoxville
sites.note: UT Knoxville university market. Oak Ridge National Lab workforce creates stable high-income demand.

sites.name: Norfolk
sites.note: Largest naval base in the world — massive military workforce housing demand. VA loan-compatible modular is a natural fit.

sites.name: Milwaukee
sites.note: WI ADU reform active. Affordable land, rust belt revival, strong union homeowner base responds to ADU-as-income framing.

sites.name: Little Rock
sites.note: AR state capital, stable government workforce. Affordable land, low competition. Establishes AR footprint.

sites.name: Des Moines
sites.note: IA ADU environment improving. Insurance/finance hub, stable employment base. Affordable land = compelling modular ROI.

sites.name: Tulsa
sites.note: OK energy/aerospace workforce. Artist District revitalization driving ADU-receptive urban buyer base. Pairs with OKC.

sites.name: El Paso
sites.note: Large military presence — Fort Bliss. TX ADU environment positive, border market creates unique workforce housing demand. Bridges TX and NM coverage.

sites.name: Madison
sites.note: UW-Madison university market, state capital stability. Progressive buyer base strongly aligned with modular/ADU values.

sites.name: Chattanooga
sites.note: Fast-growing manufacturing/tech hub. Volkswagen EV plant workforce, outdoor recreation culture drives innovation-receptive buyer. Completes TN quad.

sites.name: Lexington
sites.note: UK Lexington university market, horse farm wealth = high homeowner equity for ADU investment. Completes KY coverage.

sites.name: Bangor
sites.note: ME ADU reform, rural coverage extension. University of Maine market. Establishes northern New England presence. Low competition.

sites.name: Billings
sites.note: MT ADU environment improving. Energy sector workforce, gateway to Northern Plains markets. Largest MT city.

sites.name: Bozeman
sites.note: Fastest-growing MT city. MSU university, tech/outdoor industry migration from CA. Extreme housing cost spike — modular is natural solution.

sites.name: Fargo
sites.note: ND ADU environment improving. NDSU university, growing tech sector. Affordable land = strong modular ROI. Northern Plains presence.

sites.name: Lincoln
sites.note: UN-Lincoln university market, state capital stability. Pairs with Omaha to complete NE coverage.

sites.name: Toledo
sites.note: OH market, Jeep manufacturing workforce housing. UT Toledo university. Completes OH coverage.

sites.name: Eugene
sites.note: OR ADU law applies statewide. UO Eugene university, strong environmental buyer values. Extends Portland coverage south through the Willamette Valley.

sites.name: Bend
sites.note: OR ADU law applies. Fastest-growing OR city. Outdoor tech/remote worker migration driving extreme housing cost spike. Small market, capturable quickly.

sites.name: Sioux Falls
sites.note: SD ADU environment improving. No state income tax draws remote workers. Growing financial services sector.

sites.name: Provo
sites.note: UT ADU by-right law applies. BYU university market, high multigenerational household demand — natural ADU buyers. Young family demographic suited to modular.

sites.name: Burlington
sites.note: VT ADU legislation 2018 — statewide by-right. UVM university market, high environmental consciousness buyer. Small but high-value with strong ADU adoption culture.

sites.name: Spokane
sites.note: WA ADU reform applies statewide. Eastern WA gateway, affordable land vs Seattle, growing remote worker market.

sites.name: Cheyenne
sites.note: WY state capital, F.E. Warren AFB military presence. No state income tax attracting remote workers. Denver hub covers efficiently.

sites.name: Jackson
sites.note: MS state capital, water infrastructure investment creating construction activity. Affordable land, consistent government workforce.

sites.name: Shreveport
sites.note: LA north corridor, Barksdale AFB military workforce housing demand. Completes LA coverage with Baton Rouge and New Orleans.

sites.name: Charleston
sites.note: WV state capital, federal infrastructure investment driving new construction. Affordable land = strong modular economics. Appalachian market entry.

sites.name: Morgantown
sites.note: WVU university market, federal research workforce. Pairs with Charleston WV to complete Appalachian coverage.

sites.name: Anchorage
sites.note: Modular is essentially the only practical build method in AK. Extreme construction cost and logistics constraints make factory-built the obvious solution. Captive market. Pulled from Yr 10.

sites.name: Mobile
sites.note: AL Gulf Coast, Airbus manufacturing workforce housing demand. Port/aerospace industry. Completes AL coverage.

sites.name: Tallahassee
sites.note: FL state capital, FSU/FAMU university market. State government workforce stability. Completes FL coverage.

sites.name: Pensacola
sites.note: FL Panhandle, NAS Pensacola — enormous military workforce housing demand. VA-financeable modular natural fit.

sites.name: Hilo
sites.note: HI ADU law applies statewide. Big Island workforce housing demand. More affordable than Honolulu but same excellent policy environment.

sites.name: Fort Wayne
sites.note: IN manufacturing workforce housing. Second-largest IN city. Short haul from Indianapolis hub.

sites.name: Bismarck
sites.note: ND state capital, energy sector workforce. Pairs with Fargo to complete ND coverage.

sites.name: Concord
sites.note: NH state capital, NH ADU law applies. Completes NH coverage with Manchester.

sites.name: Allentown
sites.note: PA Lehigh Valley, manufacturing/warehouse workforce. Amazon/distribution center demand for worker housing.

sites.name: Rapid City
sites.note: SD Black Hills gateway, Ellsworth AFB military workforce housing. Pairs with Sioux Falls to complete SD.

sites.name: Laredo
sites.note: TX border market, international trade workforce housing demand. US-Mexico port of entry creates consistent housing need.

sites.name: Charlottesville
sites.note: UVA university market, high homeowner equity, educated buyer aligned with modular/ADU values. Completes VA coverage.

sites.name: Bellingham
sites.note: WA ADU reform applies. Canadian border market, WWU university, outdoor/tech culture. Extends WA coverage north from Seattle.

sites.name: Casper
sites.note: WY energy sector workforce housing demand. Completes WY coverage with Cheyenne.

sites.name: Missoula
sites.note: UM Missoula university, outdoor recreation culture = innovation-receptive buyer. Completes MT coverage with Billings and Bozeman.

sites.name: Corpus Christi
sites.note: TX Gulf Coast, NAS Corpus Christi military workforce, petrochemical industry. Completes TX coastal coverage.

sites.name: Gainesville
sites.note: UF Gainesville university market, consistent rental ADU demand. Completes FL interior coverage.

sites.name: Bakersfield
sites.note: CA ADU law applies. Oil/agriculture workforce, affordable Central Valley land. Bridges Fresno and LA Basin coverage. Large underserved housing market.

sites.name: Eureka
sites.note: CA ADU law applies. North Coast market, timber/fishing workforce, extremely limited housing supply. Completes CA coastal coverage north of Bay Area.

sites.name: Fairbanks
sites.note: AK modular essential for climate/logistics. UAF university, military (Eielson, Fort Wainwright). Completes AK coverage with Anchorage.

sites.name: Flagstaff
sites.note: AZ university market (NAU), mountain gateway, tourism workforce housing. Completes AZ triangle with Phoenix and Tucson.

sites.name: Meridian
sites.note: ID ADU-permissive. Boise suburb with fastest growth rate — modular infill is natural solution. Same excellent demand dynamics as Boise.

sites.name: Wichita
sites.note: KS aerospace manufacturing workforce (Boeing, Spirit AeroSystems). Stable, affordable market. Establishes KS presence.

sites.name: Duluth
sites.note: MN ADU reform applies. Port/manufacturing workforce, UMD university. Completes MN coverage with Minneapolis.

sites.name: Springfield
sites.note: MO Ozarks gateway, MSU Springfield university. Completes MO coverage with KC and St. Louis.

sites.name: Dothan
sites.note: AL SE corner, Wiregrass region military (Fort Rucker). Completes AL full coverage.

sites.name: Jackson
sites.note: TN west corridor, manufacturing workforce. Completes TN statewide coverage.

sites.name: Lubbock
sites.note: TX Tech university market, West TX gateway. Completes TX statewide coverage.

sites.name: Virginia Beach
sites.note: Largest VA city by population. Massive military complex (NAS Oceana, Joint Expeditionary Base). VA-financeable modular natural fit.

sites.name: Spokane Valley
sites.note: WA ADU reform applies. Pairs with Spokane for full inland WA coverage.

sites.name: Green Bay
sites.note: WI ADU reform. Manufacturing/paper industry workforce. Completes WI coverage with Milwaukee and Madison.

sites.name: Macon
sites.note: GA middle corridor, Robins AFB military workforce. Completes GA coverage with Atlanta and Savannah.

sites.name: Charleston
sites.note: WV state capital, federal infrastructure investment driving new construction.

sites.name: Waco
sites.note: TX mid-corridor, Baylor University market, chip manufacturing (Samsung Taylor nearby) creating workforce housing demand. Bridges Dallas and Austin coverage.

sites.name: Savannah South
sites.note: GA coastal coverage extension, Hyundai EV plant workforce housing demand. Completes coastal GA corridor.

sites.name: Pocatello
sites.note: ISU Pocatello university market, ID ADU-permissive. Completes ID coverage from Boise to southeast corridor.

A.name: Tier A
A.desc: Heavy regulation, seismic/coastal, high labor cost

D.name: Tier D
D.desc: South central, deep south, Appalachia, midwest cost-efficient

entitlement._doc: Sits on top of turnkey construction (factory + site work). Total = factory + site_work + land + (construction × soft_pct/100) + permits. Tune per-tier land values as you collect real bids.

cost_assumptions._documentation: Cost cascade: city.cost.psf_override > city.cost.cci * national_baseline_psf > state_defaults[state].cost_psf > national_baseline_psf. Cost stack: factory + site_work (strip%) = turnkey construction; + entitlement (land + soft + permits) = true total cost/door. blended_per_door is computed by the calibrator at render time, demand-weighted by city.cost.demand_velocity (homes/yr per fab; default 180).
